description: Evidence-store retrieval benchmark (U6, R8, KTD9). Page-labeled, modality-tagged questions drafted from parsed sample evidence 2026-07-07. Modalities: text (PDF prose), table (tabular values in PDF text), figure (visible only in the page image, not the text layer), text_native (LAS/CSV/TXT sources). 'answer' and 'evidence_basis' are for the human label review and answer-layer spot-verification; the retrieval harness reads only question/modality/expected_pages.

questions.question: On the Hooey STX Unit B N724H SLWD elemental log (18,500' MD), at what measured depths did the crew pull out of hole (POOH)?
questions.answer: 13,200 ft MD (BHA), 13,500 ft MD (poly pill), and 18,132 ft MD (BHA)

expected_pages.s3key: FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B N724H/Logs & Surveys/Mass Spec/150857_FORMENTERA_HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B-N724H_SLWD Logs_18,500'MD.pdf
FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B N724H/Logs & Surveys/Mass Spec/150857_FORMENTERA_HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B-N724H_SLWD Logs_18,500'MD.pdf

questions.evidence_basis: POOH annotations rendered on the log plot; page text layer is ~121 chars and contains none of POOH/13,200/13,500/18,132 (verified by grep). Image-only fact.

questions.question: According to the annotations on the Hooey STX Unit B N724H SLWD elemental log, what was pumped at 13,500 ft MD?
questions.answer: A poly pill (annotation: POOH@13,500'MD POLY PILL)

expected_pages.s3key: FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B N724H/Logs & Surveys/Mass Spec/150857_FORMENTERA_HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B-N724H_SLWD Logs_18,500'MD.pdf
FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B N724H/Logs & Surveys/Mass Spec/150857_FORMENTERA_HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B-N724H_SLWD Logs_18,500'MD.pdf

questions.evidence_basis: 'POLY PILL' appears nowhere in any text layer store-wide (grep: 0 hits); the annotation is image-only.

questions.question: Which formation tops are marked in the TOPS/Geoprog column of the Hooey STX Unit B N724H SLWD log?
questions.answer: GLM B, GLM C, Pearsall, U Bexar, M Bexar, L Bexar

expected_pages.s3key: FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B N724H/Logs & Surveys/Mass Spec/150857_FORMENTERA_HOOEY-STX-UNIT-B-N724H_SLWD Logs_18,500'MD.pdf

questions.evidence_basis: Colored tops column with labels visible on page 1 of the plot; absent from the text layer.

questions.question: On the Bull Mountain 31-18 DIV S611HF measured-depth log dated 2026-03-12, at approximately what shallow depth does the orange curve step sharply to the right?
questions.answer: At approximately 400 ft MD

expected_pages.s3key: WESTLAKE RESOURCES/BULL MOUNTAIN-31-18-DIV S611HF/Logs & Surveys/LWD/2026-03-12 Bull Mountain-31-18-DIV S611HF-MD 5 inch [34997727].pdf

questions.evidence_basis: Orange stepped curve visibly shifts right at ~400 ft on the page image; page text layer is 126 chars (scale numbers only) and cannot associate the step with the depth.

questions.question: On the Fonda 23-02-BAL N516HF ST2 measured-depth log, the orange curve makes its shallowest leftward deflection above 1,000 ft MD — between roughly which two depths?
questions.answer: Between approximately 380 ft and 390 ft MD (the first of a cluster of left-edge excursions spanning ~380-410 ft)

expected_pages.s3key: WESTLAKE RESOURCES/FONDA-23-02-BAL N516HF/Logs & Surveys/LWD/2026-04-18 Fonda-23-02-BAL N516HF ST2-MD 5 inch [35605513].pdf

questions.evidence_basis: Orange (rotary speed) curve makes its first hard leftward excursions at ~380-390 ft, visible on page 3 (depth range 290-490 ft); text layer is scale numbers only. Label history: originally mislabeled to the ~770-785 ft instance on page 5; the live agent found the genuinely shallowest deflection via vision during the 2026-07-07 label review, and the reviewer (Claude) verified page 3 visually and corrected the label. The agent corrected the benchmark, not the other way around.

questions.question: On the COX 2H gamma-ray MD log, around 6,420 ft MD (survey station 14), which direction does the gamma-ray curve step?
questions.answer: The curve steps to the right (higher gamma) just below station 14 at 6,420 ft

expected_pages.s3key: FP GRIFFIN/COX 2H/Logs & Surveys/LWD/COX-2H_423673403900_GR_MD.pdf

questions.evidence_basis: Curve geometry is visible only in the image; the text layer carries the survey-station annotations (values) but not the curve shape.

questions.question: Which risks does the Bull Mountain pad drilling program (Nabors B-23) associate with the instruction to perform regular flow checks?
questions.answer: NPT and stuck pipe (with excessive mud losses; proper product mix at 6 ppg noted alongside)

expected_pages.s3key: WESTLAKE RESOURCES/BULL MOUNTAIN-31-31-DIV N112DW/Drilling/Programs/FORMENTERA - DOS - BULL MOUNTAIN PAD - NABORS B-23.pdf

questions.evidence_basis: Grep 'stuck pipe' hits page 4: '...NPT, stuck pipe, excessive... Perform regular flow checks...'

questions.question: What caution does the Bull Mountain pad drilling program give about the upper and lower Bakken shale?
questions.answer: Extreme caution should be used while drilling in this section to avoid sidetracking into the upper or lower Bakken shale

expected_pages.s3key: WESTLAKE RESOURCES/BULL MOUNTAIN-31-31-DIV N112DW/Drilling/Programs/FORMENTERA - DOS - BULL MOUNTAIN PAD - NABORS B-23.pdf

questions.evidence_basis: Grep hit, page 18, Formation Considerations section.

questions.question: Per the Roper STX Unit N731HP mud program, what is the maximum recommended cuttings concentration before hole-cleaning problems are expected?
questions.answer: 6% (per the Hole Cleaning Limits graph)

expected_pages.s3key: FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/ROPER-STX N731HP/Drilling/Programs/Mud Program_Formentera_Frio County_Roper STX Unit N731HP.pdf

questions.question: What NFPA hazard diamond ratings are posted on the condensate tank photographed at the Faxel 2H facility, and which wells share the oil tank according to the handwritten note on it?
questions.answer: Flammability 4 (top/red), health 1 (left/blue), reactivity 0 (right/yellow) on the CONDENSATE placard; the handwritten note reads 2H + 3H shared oil tank.

expected_pages.s3key: FP GRIFFIN/FAXEL 2H/Well Information/Facility/IMG_2905.jpg

questions.evidence_basis: Standalone field photo (image format gate) — zero text layer; the NFPA diamond and handwritten tank note exist only in pixels. Added 2026-07-07 per Rob's label review to diversify figure evidence beyond log plots (5th distinct figure document, first standalone-image question).
